# Operational Model — 0000003-hook-based-enforcement

*Generated by spec-agent.*

---

## Overview

This feature delivers framework files (hook scripts, adapter scripts, setup scripts, SKILL.md updates). There is no deployed service, no persistent process, and no infrastructure. Operational concerns are limited to:

1. Hook script failure modes (silent, never blocking the session)
2. Telemetry backend availability (external dependency)
3. Setup script correctness across update cycles

---

## Runbook Triggers

| Trigger | Action | Owner |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Hook script exits non-zero (unexpected) | Tool may surface an error message to the user. User should re-run setup script to verify hook registration. No session impact. | User / self-service |
| gate-write.mjs blocks unexpectedly | Verify `plan/current/design.md` exists and contains a `## Component Paths` (or `## Scope`) section. If `design.md` is malformed, the gate falls back to pass-through — check the design file format. | User / self-service |
| Telemetry gaps (phase_start/end not appearing) | Check `PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL` is set and reachable. Check `PLANIFEST_SESSION_ID` is set. Verify flag files in `{tmpdir}/planifest-telemetry/` are not stale from a previous session. | User / self-service |
| OpenCode plugin not loading | Verify `opencode.json` contains the `@planifest/opencode-hooks` plugin entry. Re-run `setup/opencode.sh`. Check Bun version ≥ 1.0. | User / self-service |
| Codex CLI hooks not firing | Verify `features.codex_hooks = true` is in `.codex/config.toml`. Re-run `setup/codex-cli.sh`. Verify Codex CLI version meets minimum. | User / self-service |
| plan/current/ not archived after ship | Run ship-agent Phase 7 manually, or manually move `plan/current/` to `plan/archive/{feature-id}-{date}/`. | User / self-service |

---

## On-Call Expectations

None. This feature has no always-on service. Hook scripts are fire-and-forget. The structured-telemetry-mcp server is a separate service with its own operational model.

---

## Alerting Thresholds

Not applicable — no deployed service. Telemetry data quality monitoring (e.g. missing phase_start events) is handled by the structured-telemetry-mcp operational model, not here.

---

## Upgrade / Update

When the framework is updated (e.g. hook script changes), users re-run the appropriate `setup/{tool}.{sh,ps1}` script. All setup scripts are idempotent. Hook registration files are overwritten with the latest values on each setup run.

No database migrations, no state to preserve across upgrades. The only persistent state is:
- `.claude/settings.json` (hook registrations) — re-written by setup scripts
- `plan/current/.skips` — user data, not touched by setup scripts
- Flag files in `{tmpdir}/planifest-telemetry/` — ephemeral, per-session, OS-managed cleanup
